message:
fix(config): handle schema evolution in bandit and machine identity

Signal Bandit Forward Compatibility (suggestions/bandit/bandit.rs):
- Auto-populate missing signal type arms when loading persisted state
- When new SignalType variants are added in future versions, old
  persisted bandit.json files won't have arms for them
- On load: iterate SignalType::all() and insert default BanditArm for
  any signal types missing from the loaded data
- Prevents panics or missing arm errors when the signal enum grows
- Uses default observation_decay from BanditConfig::default()

Machine Identity Update Detection (sync/machine.rs):
- When loading existing machine identity, check if name or description
  have been overridden by the caller
- If the loaded values differ from the override parameters, update
  the identity and persist the changes
- Allows users to update machine name/description without manually
  editing the identity file
- Example: ms sync --machine-name "new-name" will update persisted
  identity if it differs from current value

Both changes follow the principle of graceful schema evolution:
existing persisted data continues to work, and updates are applied
transparently when detected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
